Title: Gene Duplication Leads to Altered Membrane Topology of a Cytochrome P450 Enzyme in Seed Plants

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports electron transfer for phenolic biosynthesis enzymes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed plausible redox partner for lumen-oriented class II CYP73 enzymes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ATR2 is discussed as an elongated N-terminus POR duplicate in vascular plants. 2. Its ER localization makes it a plausible electron donor for class II CYP73 proteins with lumen-oriented catalytic domain. 3. This role is hypothesized, not experimentally demonstrated in this paper.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-engineering target when deploying class II CYP73 topology-dependent phenylpropanoid pathways
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a primary experimental subject in Results; included because it is directly proposed as a metabolism-relevant partner for class II CYP73 function.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates trans-cinnamic acid at the 4-position to form p-coumaric acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for diverse phenolic compounds including lignin precursors and UV-protective phenolics
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane-associated enzyme family; recombinant protein assayed in yeast microsomes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in yeast; enzyme assay; metabolite profiling by HPLC
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confirms bryophyte CYP73 performs cinnamate 4-hydroxylation, supporting early land-plant phenolic metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpCYP73A48 from moss efficiently converted cinnamic acid into p-coumaric acid in yeast. 2. This demonstrates that bryophyte CYP73 already encoded a true C4H. 3. The result supports emergence of plant phenolic metabolism in bryophytes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct application in this evolutionary study; useful as an ancestral C4H reference for engineering phenylpropanoid entry reactions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome/transcriptome mining including 1kP project; accession numbers NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used to establish evolutionary origin of C4H activity rather than for applied trait engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Brachypodium distachyon; Arabidopsis thaliana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Arabidopsis cyp73a5-1 in Col-0 background; yeast WAT11
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: General phenylpropanoid entry step; class I associated with lignin precursor branch
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 family CYP73, class I
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates trans-cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides precursor pool for lignin and other phenolic compounds
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum; single N-terminal transmembrane helix with catalytic domain facing cytosol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast recombinant enzyme assays; Arabidopsis mutant complementation; EGFP localization in Nicotiana; in vitro membrane topology assays with glycosylation mapping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Efficiently catalyzes cinnamate 4-hydroxylation and restores growth of Arabidopsis cyp73a5-1 mutant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP73A92 bound cinnamate with high affinity and showed high catalytic efficiency for p-coumarate formation. 2. AtC4Hpro-driven expression complemented the severe growth defect of Arabidopsis cyp73a5-1. 3. The protein localizes to the ER and adopts a single-pass topology with the catalytic domain in the cytosol. 4. Class I topology is proposed to fit lignin-related phenylpropanoid metabolon organization.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential entry-point enzyme for engineering phenylpropanoid flux; class I form may be preferred when targeting lignin precursor production
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered phenylpropanoid flux may affect growth because C4H deficiency causes severe dwarfism
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative phylogeny from genome/transcriptome mining including 1kP; no accession listed
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Arabidopsis thaliana CYP73A5 promoter (AtC4Hpro), inducibility type NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Class I CYP73s are discussed as likely essential orthologs for lignin biosynthesis in vascular plants.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Brachypodium distachyon; Arabidopsis thaliana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Arabidopsis cyp73a5-1 in Col-0 background; yeast WAT11
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: General phenylpropanoid entry step; class I associated with lignin precursor branch
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 family CYP73, class I
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates trans-cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides precursor pool for lignin and other phenolic compounds
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum; single N-terminal transmembrane helix with catalytic domain facing cytosol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast recombinant enzyme assays; Arabidopsis mutant complementation; EGFP localization in Nicotiana; in vitro membrane topology assays for helix insertion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Efficiently catalyzes cinnamate 4-hydroxylation and rescues Arabidopsis cyp73a5-1 growth defect
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP73A93 showed high-affinity cinnamate binding and high catalytic efficiency comparable to other Brachypodium CYP73s. 2. It complemented Arabidopsis cyp73a5-1 under AtC4H promoter control. 3. It localizes to the ER and exhibits the canonical class I single-pass topology. 4. The class I architecture is inferred to support lignin-related phenylpropanoid function.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate C4H for boosting phenylpropanoid entry flux where canonical ER-facing cytosolic orientation is desired
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong perturbation of C4H affects growth; pathway redirection may alter lignin/phenolic balance
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative phylogeny from genome/transcriptome mining including 1kP; no accession listed
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Arabidopsis thaliana CYP73A5 promoter (AtC4Hpro), inducibility type NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Second Brachypodium class I paralog; highly similar to CYP73A92.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Brachypodium distachyon; Arabidopsis thaliana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Arabidopsis cyp73a5-1 in Col-0 background; yeast WAT11
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: General phenylpropanoid entry step; class II proposed to feed non-lignin phenolic branches
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 family CYP73, class II
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates trans-cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides precursor pool for flavonoids, stilbenes, coumarins, lignin-related metabolites and other phenolics
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preliminary in silico analysis suggests differential but overlapping organ expression with class I genes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ER-localized in Arabidopsis roots, cotyledons and hypocotyls when expressed from AtC4H promoter
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum; two N-terminal transmembrane helices forming an open hairpin; dual topology with catalytic domain facing either cytosol or ER lumen
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast enzyme assays; Arabidopsis cyp73a5-1 complementation; EGFP localization in Nicotiana and Arabidopsis; glycosylation-based in vitro topology mapping; membrane insertion assays; structural modeling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Maintains strong C4H activity and rescues Arabidopsis cyp73a5-1, but possesses altered ER membrane topology likely enabling specialization toward distinct phenolic branches
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP73A94 is a highly efficient cinnamate 4-hydroxylase comparable to class I enzymes in vitro. 2. It functionally complements Arabidopsis cyp73a5-1, showing canonical C4H activity in planta. 3. Despite ER localization like class I proteins, its elongated N-terminus creates a two-pass open-hairpin topology. 4. Glycosylation mapping supports dual topology with catalytic domain facing either cytosol or ER lumen. 5. The altered topology is proposed to enable specialization toward non-lignin phenolic pathways or secretory-route phenolics.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering phenylpropanoid branch partitioning or secretory phenolic production because topology may alter metabolon membership without changing catalytic chemistry
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May redirect flux away from lignin-associated branches; possible altered interactions with redox partners and pathway complexes
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Potential redox partner ATR2 proposed but not experimentally validated
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative phylogeny from genome/transcriptome mining including 1kP; no accession listed
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Arabidopsis thaliana CYP73A5 promoter (AtC4Hpro); CaMV 35S for transient EGFP fusion expression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Class II proteins contain a conserved insertion near the heme-binding cysteine on the proximal surface, possibly linked to alternative electron donor interaction.
